content:
Key Findings

  • MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS received £31.5M from Preston City Council, accounting for 15.3% of total spending.
  • The top 5 suppliers, including MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS, received £82.2M, or 39.9% of total spend.
  • Over the 7-year period, Preston City Council spent £205.9M across 46,711 transactions.
  • LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL was the second-largest supplier, receiving £28.0M (13.6% of total spend).
  • The top 10 suppliers combined received £100.9M, representing 49.0% of total spending.

Preston's Largest Supplier: MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS

MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS stands out as Preston City Council's largest supplier, receiving £31.5M over the 7-year period analysed. This substantial amount represents 15.3% of the council's total spending of £205.9M. The relationship between the council and MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS is significant, given that no other single supplier received a comparable amount.

The scale of this spending raises questions about the nature of the services or goods provided by MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS. While the data does not specify the exact nature of these transactions, the consistent and substantial payments suggest a long-term contract or ongoing service agreement. Understanding the specifics of this relationship could provide valuable insights into the council's priorities and spending decisions.

Top Suppliers: A Concentration of Spending

The data reveals a concentration of spending among a small number of suppliers. The top 5 suppliers, including MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS and LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL, received a combined total of £82.2M, which is 39.9% of the total spending. This concentration continues with the top 10 suppliers, who received £100.9M, or 49.0% of the total spend.

The remaining 36,701 transactions, involving suppliers outside the top 10, accounted for £105.0M, or 51.0% of the total spending. This distribution highlights the council's reliance on a relatively small number of key suppliers for a significant portion of its expenditures.

Comparing the Top Suppliers

To better understand the spending patterns, it is useful to compare the amounts received by the top suppliers. The table below lists the top 10 suppliers and their respective shares of the total spending.

Rank Supplier Amount % of Total
1 MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS £31.5M 15.3%
2 LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL £28.0M 13.6%
3 CONLON CONSTRUCTION LTD £10.6M 5.2%
4 ERIC WRIGHT CIVIL ENGINEERING LTD £6.7M 3.2%
5 ZURICH MUNICIPAL £5.3M 2.6%
6 MATRIX SCM LTD £4.6M 2.2%
7 SOFTCAT LTD £4.2M 2.0%
8 PRESTON YOUTH ZONE LTD £3.6M 1.7%
9 FAUN ZOELLER (UK) LTD £3.2M 1.6%
10 STANDARD FUEL OILS LTD £3.1M 1.5%

The table clearly shows the dominance of MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS and LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL in the council's spending. The significant amounts received by these suppliers suggest that they play crucial roles in the council's operations and service delivery.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Preston City Council about the nature of the services or goods provided by MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to learn more about the council's spending decisions and supplier relationships.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see the details of contracts awarded to MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS and other top suppliers.
  • Write to your local councillor to express any concerns or questions about the council's spending patterns and supplier relationships.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and gain a deeper understanding of Preston City Council's financial activities.
This article is based on Preston City Council's published spending data covering 2019/20 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
